script:
It is early.
The room is dark.
You are already behind.

Last night you swore you would ship the OATH v1 spec to GitHub
and send Oliver the link before noon.
That was not a wish.
That was a contract.

Kobe was in the gym at 4am.
Not because the doors opened early.
Because he was already there.
No crowd. No camera. Eight hundred makes before the sun came up.
The work happened before anyone else started their day.

This is the same.

Trust the work.

Sixty minutes. Phone face down.
Open the spec doc.
Write the wedge section.
One section becomes the whole.
The whole becomes the link.
The link goes to Oliver before noon.

Nothing else exists until the wedge section is done.

Write.

attempts.user_prompt_used:
Generate the OATH ritual script using:

mode: hardest_work
variant: default
user's intent (the hardest task they swore last night they'd tackle this morning): "ship the OATH v1 spec to github and send Oliver the link before noon"
user's first action (what they'll do in the first 5 minutes): "open the spec doc and write the wedge section"
user's hero (reference by concrete habit, never by invented quote): Kobe Bryant
user's grounding phrase (use verbatim, do not paraphrase): "trust the work"

Target read duration: 60 seconds (~150 words at 150 wpm).

Output as JSON:
{
  "script": "the spoken text, with line breaks for natural pauses",
  "estimated_duration_seconds": <integer>,
  "word_count": <integer>
}

Output the JSON only. No preamble. No explanation. No markdown fence.
